crossChain.title: Cross-Chain Price Convergence Analysis
crossChain.deviationAlert: ALERT: {count} chain(s) exhibiting price divergence exceeding acceptable variance threshold - arbitrage opportunity or oracle latency detected

tooltip.averagePrice: Average of all chain prices, reflecting overall price level
tooltip.medianPrice: Median is the middle value when prices are sorted, more robust than mean
tooltip.highestPrice: Highest and lowest prices among all chains
tooltip.priceRange: Difference between highest and lowest prices, reflecting price volatility range
tooltip.iqr: IQR = Q3 - Q1, reflects the dispersion of price distribution
tooltip.standardDeviation: Standard deviation measures dispersion relative to the mean
tooltip.skewness: Skewness measures symmetry of price distribution, positive means right-skewed
tooltip.kurtosis: Kurtosis measures peakedness of price distribution, positive means more peaked than normal
tooltip.confidenceInterval95: 95% CI means there's 95% probability the true price falls within this range
tooltip.coefficientOfVariation: Coefficient of Variation = StdDev/Mean, for comparing dispersion across different scales
tooltip.consistencyRating: Consistency rating based on standard deviation percentage
tooltip.dataPoints: Total number of historical price data points

crossChain.acfExplanation: ACF Chart: If autocorrelation coefficients exceed confidence intervals (red dashed line), residuals show autocorrelation. Ljung-Box test p-value < 0.05 indicates significant autocorrelation.
crossChain.distributionExplanation: Distribution: Compare empirical distribution with normal distribution. Large differences may violate normality assumptions.
crossChain.residualsHaveAutocorrelation: Residuals show autocorrelation, model may need improvement.
crossChain.residualsNoAutocorrelation: Residuals show no significant autocorrelation, model fits well.
crossChain.pearsonCorrelationDesc: Pearson correlation coefficient measures linear correlation between two chain price trends. r = 1 means perfect positive correlation, r = -1 means perfect negative correlation, r = 0 means no linear correlation.
crossChain.volatilityConeDesc: Shows volatility distribution range across different time windows to assess current volatility level
crossChain.fixedThresholdDesc: Use fixed percentage as anomaly detection threshold
crossChain.dynamicThresholdDesc: Dynamically adjust threshold based on historical volatility (CV)
crossChain.atrThresholdDesc: Use ATR (Average True Range) indicator to calculate dynamic threshold
crossChain.cointegrationDesc: Cointegration analysis detects long-term equilibrium relationships between two chain prices. ADF statistic less than critical value (-2.86) indicates cointegration exists.
crossChain.volatilityAnalysisDesc: Analyze volatility levels across chains to identify high and low volatility periods.
crossChain.needMoreData: More historical data required for testing
crossChain.longSpreadAdvice: Spread is at historical low, recommend long spread (buy undervalued asset, sell overvalued asset), expecting spread to revert to mean.
crossChain.shortSpreadAdvice: Spread is at historical high, recommend short spread (sell overvalued asset, buy undervalued asset), expecting spread to revert to mean.
crossChain.neutralAdvice: Spread is within reasonable range, recommend waiting for better entry opportunity.
crossChain.heatmapHint: Click any cell to pin comparison. Hover for detailed spread information.

stakingDistribution.title: Validator Staking Distribution
stakingDistribution.nakamotoDesc: Only {count} validators need to collude to control 33.3% of stake
stakingDistribution.hhiDesc: Lower HHI indicates more dispersed staking (ideal < 1500)
stakingDistribution.concentrationWarningDesc: Network staking is highly concentrated. The top {count} validators can control 33.3% of the stake, posing a security risk.
stakingDistribution.concentrationReminderDesc: Network staking distribution has some concentration risk. It is recommended to continuously monitor validator staking changes.
stakingDistribution.nakamotoExplanation: Nakamoto Coefficient: How many validators need to collude to control 33.3% of stake. Higher is safer.
stakingDistribution.hhiExplanation: Herfindahl Index (HHI): Measures staking concentration. Below 1500 indicates dispersion, above 2500 indicates high concentration.
stakingDistribution.topNExplanation: Top N Ratio: The total stake ratio of top N validators. Top 3 should not exceed 33%.

explanation.title: Volatility Calculation Explanation
explanation.stdDevDesc: Standard Deviation (σ): Measures price deviation from the mean, σ = √[Σ(xi-x̄)² / n]
explanation.cvDesc: Coefficient of Variation (CV): Ratio of standard deviation to mean, CV = (σ / x̄) × 100%
explanation.cvInterpretation: Lower CV indicates more stable prices, higher CV indicates greater volatility
explanation.rollingWindowDesc: Rolling volatility uses a 5-point window to reflect volatility trend over time
explanation.multiScaleDesc: Multi-scale analysis helps identify volatility characteristics across different periods
explanation.decompositionDesc: Volatility decomposition shows the contribution of short, mid, and long-term volatility to total volatility

explanation.title: Pearson Correlation Coefficient Explanation
explanation.description: Pearson correlation coefficient measures the linear correlation between two oracle price series. The coefficient ranges from -1 to 1, where 1 indicates perfect positive correlation, -1 indicates perfect negative correlation, and 0 indicates no linear correlation. High correlation coefficients indicate that oracle price movements are highly consistent and data quality is reliable.

oraclePageTemplate.ecosystemDescription: {name} ecosystem covers {count} blockchain networks, providing secure and reliable data services for DeFi, NFT, gaming and more.

crossChainPriceConsistency.title: Cross-Chain Price Consistency Monitor
crossChainPriceConsistency.warningDesc: Some chains show significant deviation from the base price (Solana). Please monitor price consistency.
crossChainPriceConsistency.pythMechanism1: Solana serves as Pyth's main chain, prices are first published on Solana
crossChainPriceConsistency.pythMechanism2: Through Wormhole cross-chain messaging, prices are synced to Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, Optimism, Polygon and other chains
crossChainPriceConsistency.pythMechanism3: Prices across chains should remain highly consistent, deviation typically less than 0.1%
crossChainPriceConsistency.pythMechanism4: Large deviations may indicate network congestion or cross-chain message delays

redStoneMetrics.title: RedStone Core Metrics
redStoneMetrics.dataLayerDesc: Independent data provider network delivering high-quality price data through signed data packages
redStoneMetrics.onDemandPullDesc: Pull-based data model, fetching data only when needed, significantly reducing Gas costs
redStoneMetrics.dataVerificationDesc: All data is multi-signature verified to ensure accuracy and tamper-resistance

latency.title: Price Update Latency Trend
latency.pythNotSupported: Pyth API does not support historical price queries, using mock data
latency.fetchError: Unable to fetch latency data, using mock data
latency.predictionDescription: Number of future prediction data points
latency.baselineDesc: Moving average of last 20 data points, representing current latency baseline
latency.stdDevDesc: Measures latency data volatility, 2σ covers ~95% of normal data
latency.aboutDesc: Price update latency reflects the time from Pyth Network oracle fetching price updates from data sources to on-chain availability. Normal latency should stay below 200ms. When latency spikes abnormally, it may indicate network congestion, slow data source response, or high system load. Monitor anomaly periods and assess impact on applications.

bollinger.insufficientData: Insufficient data, at least {period} data points required

dataQuality.accuracyDesc: Price data deviation from reference value
dataQuality.consistencyDesc: Stability and continuity of data updates
dataQuality.heartbeatComplianceDesc: Ratio of data updates meeting expected heartbeat
dataQuality.qualityScoreDescTitle: Quality score is calculated based on the following dimensions:
dataQuality.outlierDetectionDesc: Identify data points significantly deviating from normal range

disputeVoting.statusQuorumReached: Voting in Progress - Quorum reached but threshold not met
disputeVoting.statusInProgress: Voting in Progress - Waiting for more validators

diaRiskAssessment.decentralizationMetricDesc: Multi-source data aggregation, covering centralized exchanges and DeFi protocols
diaRiskAssessment.securityMetricDesc: Multi-layer data verification mechanism, open-source transparent codebase
diaRiskAssessment.dataQualityMetricDesc: Real-time data updates, anomaly detection and cleansing mechanism
diaRiskAssessment.eventDIAUpgradeDesc: Enhanced data verification layer, introducing zero-knowledge proof verification mechanism
diaRiskAssessment.eventAnomalyResponseDesc: Quickly identify and isolate abnormal exchange data sources, ensuring data accuracy
diaRiskAssessment.eventBridgeMaintenanceDesc: Regular security audits and cross-chain message passing optimization
diaRiskAssessment.eventDashboardReleaseDesc: Launch real-time data source transparency monitoring panel

forms.selectExportFormatDescription: Select the format to export. CSV is suitable for data analysis, PNG for sharing, and JSON for programmatic processing.
